text: During the laparoscopic appendectomy for suppurative acute appendicitis, pus is visible in the right paracolic gutter and pelvis. The appendix is amalgamated with the terminal ileum and cecum, forming an appendicular mass. The surgeon uses blunt dissection with an aspirator to separate the appendix from surrounding structures. The surgical field appears hyperemic. A Maryland Dissector with electrocautery is used on the mesoappendix, which is tough and bloody. Further blunt dissection with the Melanin Dissector is performed to reach the proper base of the appendix.

The surgeon uses a grasper to hold the appendix and a knot pusher to slide a knot at its base. The thread is cut, and the appendix is extracted through the umbilical port. A 5mm lens is used for visualization, followed by irrigation and aspiration, and a drain is inserted.
